doc.title: Bovin · AI Quant Trading System
title.main: Bovin AI quant trading system (Bovin · Bovin AI)
panel.apiCatalog: Backend endpoints (common & panel-related; not full OpenAPI)
monitor.title: System Monitor
monitor.subtitle: Host, browser environment, and link metrics (some are limited by browser/API).
monitor.hint.disk: Stock Bovin does not expose disk; live shows N/A; mock uses a demo value.
monitor.hint.bandwidth: Network Information API — client estimate, not datacenter or exchange throughput.
monitor.hint.cagr: From GET /profit field cagr (see Bovin docs for meaning).
settings.baseUrlPlaceholder: Local dev: http://127.0.0.1:19090/api/v1 (run frontend/binance_proxy.py); same-origin deploy can stay empty
settings.apiCredsFromLoginHint: Bovin REST HTTP Basic uses the same username and password as panel login (must match api_server in config). After changing either password, sign in again.
settings.wsApiV1Placeholder: Empty: when REST uses :19090 proxy, WS base is same host with :18080/api/v1 (127.0.0.1 locally, LAN IPv4 when opened by IP)
settings.wsTokenOptional: Must match api_server.ws_token; stored in this browser only
settings.wsTokenHint: Optional: the panel still works via HTTP polling. After you set api_server.ws_token and save, the top bar shows live push and updates get faster.
live.rpcBadgeTitleOn: Subscribed to RPC over WebSocket; overview and positions refresh faster.
live.rpcBadgeTitleNoToken: No ws_token: using timed HTTP polling (not a push failure). Add api_server.ws_token in Settings and save.
live.rpcBadgeTitleWsDown: ws_token set but WebSocket is down; fell back to polling. Check Bovin :18080 reachability, firewall, and token.
settings.prefsLoadFailed: Could not load settings (GET /panel/preferences failed). API bindings, strategy preferences, and the users list need a working REST path before they appear.
settings.prefs401Hint: 401/403: use the same api_server username/password (HTTP Basic). After a successful login the panel stores short-lived tokens in localStorage so new tabs can refresh access automatically; if token exchange never succeeded or refresh expired, sign in again.
settings.prefs404Hint: 404: the REST base must end with …/api/v1 (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:18080/api/v1). Host:port without /api/v1 hits the wrong path.
settings.prefsLoadFailedUpstreamNote: With the local :19090 proxy: the proxy received the request but could not reach Bovin upstream (FREQTRADE_UPSTREAM, often http://127.0.0.1:18080). Start Bovin with api_server or fix the upstream URL; the SQLite file is usually fine.
settings.bindingsUnavailableUpstream: Cannot load API bindings (server unreachable). This does not mean the database is empty—fix the REST/upstream issue above, then refresh.
settings.apiBindingsStaleCacheBanner: Showing the last successfully synced API mappings (cached in this tab). Connection to the database is unverified—confirm the server is healthy before saving to avoid overwriting the DB.
settings.showConfigShortUpstreamDup: Same cause as above: Bovin upstream is down or FREQTRADE_UPSTREAM / Base URL is wrong.
settings.panelUsersUpstreamOnly: Cannot load users: REST upstream unreachable (often Bovin :18080 not running or proxy misconfigured).
users.title: Users & menu permissions
users.passwordOptional: Leave blank to keep current password
users.readonlyHint: Your account has read-only access to Settings; user management is disabled.
users.lastUserError: At least one panel user is required.
users.confirmDelete: Delete this user? This cannot be undone.
login.error.apiAuth: Use the same username and password as api_server in config (HTTP Basic + panel login). After Bovin starts, a matching panel user is synced automatically. Check Base URL is reachable.
settings.controlShowStrategyCards: Show strategy card list on Strategy AI console
settings.controlShowStrategyCardsHint: Applies to this entry only: when off, this strategy shows a short note on the console instead of the full multi-card grid from /panel/strategies.
settings.strategyListLead: Use New to add a class name in a dialog; each row below can be edited or deleted.
settings.strategyEntryRowClickHint: Click this row or press Enter to open the dialog and edit the full strategy
settings.strategyNameRequired: Enter a strategy name / class name.
settings.manualStrategiesHint: For names not yet returned by /panel/strategies; the console shows a card (JSON/MML) for each. Does not create .py files; saved with panel preferences.
settings.manualStrategiesEmpty: No entries yet. Click New above to add one in the dialog.
settings.confirmRemoveManualStrategy: Remove this entry from the list? Its JSON/MML slot data will be deleted too.
settings.manualStrategyDup: That name is already in the list.
settings.manualStrategyShowOnConsoleHint: Applies to this entry: when on, show this strategy's card on the console (JSON/MML). You can keep a class name here even if not yet returned by /panel/strategies.
settings.confirmClearMemoField: Clear “{field}”? This cannot be undone from here except by re-entering.
llmProbe.status.testing: Probing exchange and LLM via server…
modal.strategyConfigSub: Stored in panel_store.sqlite. Each strategy is one entry; the first entry mirrors the primary panel summary.
modal.strategySlotSub: JSON and/or MML: one, both, or neither. Empty is OK for condition/AI-driven runs; saved with panel preferences.
modal.manualStrategyEntrySub: Panel list and slot data only; does not create .py files on the server.
hint.strategyFileManual: To turn takeover-era logic into a reusable main strategy: distill rules from your operation/decision logs (or use an LLM to draft code), then manually save under user_data/strategies and iterate. This panel only stores notes and rule text — it does not write .py strategy files automatically.
hint.strategySlotEither: Unlike the global “Extra JSON” in Strategy config, this is stored per strategy card for orchestration or external tools.
label.strategyRpcBaseHint: Leave empty to use the global Base URL in Settings. When set to a full …/api/v1 URL, this strategy card talks only to that Bovin trade instance for /start, /show_config, etc. Run one trade process per port with its own config/DB; enable CORS for your browser origin on each. Same API credentials as the global connection.
label.aiTakeoverTradingHint: Skip classic strategy path; AI-managed trading (panel flag — needs external orchestration)
label.paperTrading: Paper trading (AI only, no trade API)
label.paperTradingHint: LLM and other AI endpoints in bindings still work; the console will not call bot trade APIs (/start, /pause, …). “Sync and start” only saves panel prefs and does not POST /start.
label.riskUseFreqaiLimitsHint: Prefer Bovin AI max_training_drawdown_pct for the drawdown cap line (vs manual % below)
placeholder.strategyAuthorizedAmount: Optional; unit is the stake currency shown beside the label
label.strategyRulesOutput: Rule output (AI sessions / manual; append via API POST /panel/strategy-rules/append)
page.desc.overview: Bot status, host metrics and logs — all from your local Bovin instance (some KPIs are visual placeholders)
page.desc.positions: Live positions, floating P&L and open orders
page.desc.control: Start / pause / stop, reload config and lists; some cards are UI mocks
page.desc.settings: API bindings, strategy notes, and user permissions — credentials and preferences stay on this machine.
page.desc.monitor: Host resources, browser environment, and link metrics (some depend on browser and API capabilities).
page.desc.api: Endpoint catalog aligned with Bovin REST, plus panel action results.
login.title: Bovin
login.error: Invalid credentials. Use api_server credentials; admin/admin is only the legacy default when api_server has no username/password.
login.reauthRequired: You still looked signed in, but the API password is missing (passwords are kept in this tab’s session only—new tabs or restarts require signing in again). Re-enter the same username and password as api_server in config.
aria.strategySlotNameSelect: Choose strategy class for slot editing
mock.toggleMockAria: Toggle mock or live data, currently
overview.risk.title: Risk exposure (crypto buckets)
topbar.notify.title: Alerts · recent logs
topbar.notify.empty: No log lines yet. They appear here after the overview refresh pulls /logs.
topbar.notify.mockHint: Mock mode: placeholder only. Connect live bot to see /logs snippets here.
topbar.account.title: Session
topbar.hint.network: Bovin REST / ping reachability and RTT — not a Web3 wallet or chain node.
topbar.hint.market: Caption follows the exchange from bot /show_config — not an on-chain DEX status.
topbar.hint.systemBadge: Online/offline hint from the last API probe.
overview.strategies.title: Active strategies
overview.strategies.empty: No active strategies (bot is not running or the trade process is not loaded).
sc.tradeMode.bannerTitle: Webserver mode: the panel cannot start/pause live trading here
sc.tradeMode.bannerBody: Run Bovin in trading mode: from repo root run `./scripts/start_panel_dev.sh` (defaults to trade), or `python -m bovin trade -c user_data/config.json -c user_data/config.api.json`. Webserver mode serves data/backtest APIs only; it does not expose POST /start, /pause, or /stop. For a split setup (webserver for data, trade for execution), set env BOVIN_TRADE_UPSTREAM on binance_proxy to the trade API origin (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:18080) and keep BOVIN_UPSTREAM pointing at webserver.
sc.tradeMode.splitRelayTitle: Split upstream: trading API is proxied to the trade process
sc.tradeMode.splitRelayBody: show_config stays webserver; /start, /pause, positions, etc. go to: {url}
sc.tradeMode.splitRelayBodyImplicit: show_config stays webserver; trading APIs are routed to the trade process via the local proxy (live /api/v1/health). If the proxy root /health did not expose bovin_trade_upstream, the panel still enables controls from the health payload.
sc.tradeMode.actionsBlocked: Trading controls are unavailable in webserver mode; run `bovin trade` or set BOVIN_TRADE_UPSTREAM on the local proxy.
sc.tradeMode.syncStartBlocked: Backend is webserver without a trade upstream; cannot POST /start from here.
sc.tradeMode.paperActionsBlocked: This strategy is marked paper-only: bot trade APIs are disabled; AI / binding config still applies.
sc.tradeMode.forceEnterBlocked: Backend is webserver without a trade upstream; force-enter is disabled.
sc.tradeMode.apiErrorShort: Trading mode (dry_run or live) required. Detail:
sc.strategy.inactiveHelp: Not enabled in config.json. Change strategy there and reload.
sc.strategy.noFiles: No strategy cards yet. Set Strategy name / preference under Settings → Strategy config; the list merges that name, the bot config strategy, and server scan results.
sc.strategy.listHidden: Strategy cards are hidden in Settings. Turn them on under Settings → Strategy config.
sc.strategy.entryGridOff: “{name}”: full strategy cards are off on the console
sc.strategy.entryGridOffHint: Edit this entry under Settings → Strategy config and enable “Show strategy card list on Strategy AI console”.
sc.strategy.allVisibleGridOffLead: Every visible strategy has full console cards turned off; only short notes are shown:
sc.strategy.allHiddenByPref: Nothing is shown on the console for visible strategies. Edit the entries in Settings → Strategy config and enable “Show on console”.
sc.demo.banner: Visual mock only — not real Bovin state; exchange / slippage text is for layout demo.
sc.gov.title: Asset access governance
sc.gov.autoBlBody: Pairs with 24h volume under 1M USDT or spread over 2% are restricted from execution.
sc.feed.title: Live execution feed
settings.showConfigHintRefused: — The browser reached your Base URL (e.g. local :19090 dev proxy), but the proxy could not open a TCP connection to the Bovin REST upstream (env FREQTRADE_UPSTREAM, often http://127.0.0.1:18080). Start Bovin with api_server on that host/port, or fix FREQTRADE_UPSTREAM / Base URL. This is usually not a wrong API password (wrong creds are typically 401).
settings.showConfigHintGeneric: Check Base URL and network. 401 means HTTP Basic does not match api_server in config: sign in again with those credentials (do not use legacy admin unless your API user is actually admin).
modal.apiBindingSub: Secrets are stored in local panel and server user_data; never enter production keys on untrusted networks.
modal.api.exchangePassphraseHintDefault: Leave blank for most spot APIs; fill only if the exchange issued a separate passphrase.
modal.api.exchangePassphraseHintBinance: Binance spot and futures APIs usually need no passphrase unless you set one in the API management UI.
modal.api.exchangePassphraseHintPass: This exchange often requires a passphrase / API password—match what you set on the exchange.
api.hint.panelPost501: Not from Binance docs: POST never reached Bovin (often the static panel server). Cleared stale “last OK base” and retried once; if it still fails, set Bovin Base URL to http://127.0.0.1:19090/api/v1 and run python3 frontend/binance_proxy.py.
modal.api.bindingEnabledHint: When off, this row is ignored for sync and effective configuration.
modal.api.bindToBot: On save, sync this row to bot config (exchange block)
modal.api.bindToBotHint: Only one row can be selected. Writes the main on-disk config’s exchange section; restart trade/webserver to reload in-process settings.
settings.exchangeSyncFailed: Failed to write bot exchange config
kline.noData: Live data unreachable — no candles to render
kline.malformedRows: Candle response received but OHLC rows are invalid
kline.cdnFallback: CDN unreachable — using local fallback chart (auto-retry)
log.csvNoData: No history to export — load Pair History first.
log.webserverMode: Webserver mode: trade/position endpoints are limited by design.
log.webserverModeTradeHint: To use Start strategy and other bot controls in the panel, run a trading process: `bovin trade` (not webserver).
log.syncStartPaperSkipped: Panel prefs saved; primary strategy is paper-only — /start was not called (no trade API).
log.logsHint: See Recent Logs in overview or API panel.
log.tuneLocalSvg: Local SVG candles on (OHLC still from current source, e.g. COINBASE); tap tune again for the chart library
overview.sysinfo.hint404: The API returned 404 (often an HTML page from a static server). Set Base URL to your bot’s REST root ending in /api/v1, not the front-end-only port.
overview.telemetry.loadAvg.hint.busy: high but still under core count
overview.telemetry.loadAvg.hint.heavy: at or above core count — saturated
overview.telemetry.friendly.cpuAvgVal: About {{pct}}%. Average busy level across all cores.
overview.telemetry.friendly.cpuCountVal: {{n}} cores — rough scale for how much work can run in parallel.
